title: Red Sanders (Red Sandalwood)
focus: Habitat, characteristics, uses, and conservation status of Red Sanders.
rationale: Highly examinable due to its IUCN Endangered status, CITES Appendix II listing, and specific endemic habitat in the Eastern Ghats.
sectionExcerpt: National Biodiversity Authority sanctioned fund for Red Sanders Conservation under Access and Benefit Sharing (ABS) Mechanism. Native to Southern Eastern Ghats of Andhra Pradesh. Grows in dry deciduous forests between 150-900 meters altitude. Presence of red dye 'Santalin' imparts its characteristic red colour. Conservation Status: CITES Appendix II; IUCN Status: EN (Endangered).
evidenceBullets: Native to the Southern Eastern Ghats of Andhra Pradesh.
IUCN Status is Endangered (EN) and listed in CITES Appendix II.

title: Banana Musa Indandamanensis
focus: Discovery, physical characteristics, and genetic significance of the wild banana species Musa indandamanensis.
rationale: Notable for its unique physical characteristics (longest infructescence, 11m height) and specific geographic origin in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
sectionExcerpt: An infructescence of about 4.2 metres recorded in species of wild banana Musa indandamanensis, making it the longest infructescence recorded in bananas. First recorded from a remote tropical forest near Krishna Nala reserve forest in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in 2012. Unique green flowers; fruit bunch lux (axis) thrice size of regular banana species. 11 metres high. Natural genetic resource for plant breeders to develop high yielding and disease resistant varieties.
evidenceBullets: Discovered in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in 2012.
Has the longest recorded infructescence in bananas at about 4.2 metres.
Grows up to 11 metres high and features unique green flowers.
Acts as a natural genetic resource for developing disease-resistant plant varieties.

title: White Himalayan Lily
focus: Characteristics, medicinal uses, and conservation status of the White Himalayan Lily.
rationale: Important for UPSC due to its Critically Endangered (CR) IUCN status and specific medicinal properties.
sectionExcerpt: Uttarakhand Forest Department’s latest report highlights conservation of White Himalayan Lily (Lilium polyphyllum). Rare and fragrant lily species native to Himalayan region. Known for white or pink flowers with dark spots and stripes. Rich in active chemical constituents like alkaloids and furocoumarins. Used for treating haematemesis, bronchitis, rheumatism, etc. IUCN Status: Critically Endangered (CR).
evidenceBullets: Rich in active chemical constituents like alkaloids and furocoumarins.
Used medicinally for treating haematemesis, bronchitis, and rheumatism.

title: Murikooti Pacha
focus: Distribution, traditional names, and medicinal properties of Murikooti Pacha.
rationale: A specific medicinal plant with a unique active compound (acteoside) and traditional significance (Vranaropani) that can be tested in science and technology or environment sections.
sectionExcerpt: Indian researchers harnessed its wound-healing potential. Native to Southeast Asia, also found in other tropical regions including India. Red ivy plant, also known as Vranaropani in Sanskrit. Wound-healing pad containing, acteoside, a natural compound known for therapeutic potential.
evidenceBullets: Native to Southeast Asia and found in tropical regions including India.
Contains acteoside, a natural compound with significant wound-healing and therapeutic potential.
